text.title: match-real-world Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for match-real-world as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Users have no context for internal terminology; it creates a barrier to entry.

failure-mode.description: Technical language: Developer/system terminology in user-facing copy
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Not all users are technical; the feature must be usable by the broadest intended audience.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Abbreviations require recall; spelled-out terms are more discoverable in search and easier to understand.

failure-mode.description: Inconsistent terms: Same thing called different names
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Inconsistency forces users to recall multiple labels for the same concept; terminology should be standardized.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Metaphors and conventions vary by culture; direct translation of English terminology often produces awkward or misleading text.

failure-mode.description: Agents rewriting technical terms often change meaning subtly
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: "Repository" replaced with "folder" loses the version-control concept. Require the agent to flag ambiguous replacements.

failure-mode.description: Plain-language alternatives generated for generic instructions may not match the product's specific feature names
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Provide a product glossary as context.

failure-mode.description: Error message rewrites default to overly apologetic tone
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents default to "We're so sorry something went wrong!" — specify the desired tone explicitly in the prompt.

failure-mode.description: Agents do not know what terms your specific user segment actually uses
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without interview or support ticket data as input, they will use generic consumer-app vocabulary. Provide research data as context.

failure-mode.description: Publishing agent-generated terminology without human review
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Human checkpoint required before terminology changes are deployed — wrong plain-language replacements erode trust faster than technical jargon.
